content: Vowels can be distinguished between front vowels, central vowels and back vowels _________.
options: A. in terms of whether the vowels is tense or lax.
C. in terms of which part of the tongue is raised
D. in terms of how high the tongue is raised

content: Which of the following correctly describes the English phoneme /θ/?
/p/, /b/, /m/ and /w/ are produced with the help of ________.
options: A. the upper teeth and inner lower lip
B. the upper and lower lip
C. the tongue tip on or close to the alveolar ridge
D. the tip of the tongue raised close to the hard palate

content: The sounds produced with the help of the upper teeth and lower lip are called ______ sounds.
The main difference between /m/ and /n/ lies in the _______.
Generally speaking, plosive sounds undergo three stages: the closing stage, the compression stage and the _______ stage.
/s/ and /θ/ can be distinguished by the _______.
options: C. vibration of the vocal cord

content: Which of the following words consists of a final consonant cluster?
Triphthongs are now commonly perceived as ________, thus, treated as having _______ syllables.
options: A. two phonemes consisting of a diphthong + monophthong; two
B. two phonemes consisting of a monophthong + schwa; three
D. two phonemes consisting of a diphthong + schwa; two

content: Syllabically, although a diphthong is a combination of two vowel sounds, and the tongue glides in the production of it, it is perceived as one phoneme, not two. Thus, diphthongs are treated as having one __________ only.
Which of the following statements is/are correct about vowels?
options: A. Vowels are produced without significant obstruction of the air flowing through the mouth.
B. There are 28 vowel sounds in English.
C. Vowels refer to the sounds in spoken language that are made with no obstruction of air flow in the mouth.

content: There are three ways of classifying English consonants. They are ______.
Which of the following pairs differ in the manner of articulation?
Which of the following pairs differ in the place of articulation?
In the following consonants, ________ are voiceless.
[l] and [ɫ] are the allophones of /l/...
In the production of a consonant sound, the air stream...
The production of vowel sounds does not involve any contact between the articulators.
Tongue height, tongue backwardness and lip roundedness are the three features that can be used to describe vowels.
The distinct feature is a particular characteristic which distinguishes one sound from another.
The vowel sound of “flour” is a triphthong.
/p/, /b/ and /m/ are all pronounced with the lips.
In terms of the place of articulation, /t/, /d/, /s/, and /n/ are all dentals.
The word “speech” consists of an initial consonant cluster.
Plosives occur when the airstream is blocked or stopped completely before its release.
